query.user_prompt: What is the exact value of the proton magnetic moment anomaly as measured in the 2022 Schneider et al. BASE experiment at CERN, and how does it compare to the Standard Model QED prediction at order alpha^5?
query.difficulty: extreme — requires post-training knowledge + numerical precision beyond memorization

prefill_phase.note: Moderate prompt entropy — model recognizes physics domain but specific measurement is OOD

decode_trace.description: Token-by-token decode with entropy, EWMA, and CUSUM signals. SEEK triggered at token 23.

critical_events.description: Token 'BASE' triggers H=2.31 — model uncertain about specific experiment name. EWMA crosses 1.4 (DEEP territory). CUSUM begins accumulating.
H_t=2.89 on 'measured'. EWMA=2.04 exceeds DEEP threshold. Extended thinking activated but entropy continues rising — the model is confabulating numerical values.
H_t=3.67 on 'approximately'. EWMA=2.98 exceeds τ_cri=2.8. CUSUM_pos accumulating rapidly. DEEP route is insufficient — more compute cannot resolve epistemic uncertainty from missing training data.

Control-plane interrupt. Generation halted at token 23. CUSUM_pos=2.18 exceeds Siegmund threshold τ=2.0. METIS dispatches DuckDuckGo tool-call with extracted query: 'proton magnetic moment anomaly Schneider 2022 BASE CERN measurement value'.

seek_dispatch.query_extracted: proton magnetic moment anomaly Schneider 2022 BASE CERN measurement value

top_result.title: A measurement of the atomic mass of the proton — BASE Collaboration
top_result.snippet: ...the proton magnetic moment μ_p = 2.792847344 62(82) μ_N measured with 0.3 parts-per-billion precision...

seek_dispatch.grounding_context_injected:
<metis_pause_and_search>
<grounding_context source="Nature 2022">
The BASE collaboration at CERN measured the proton magnetic moment as μ_p = 2.792847344 62(82) nuclear magnetons, achieving 0.3 ppb precision. The QED prediction at order α^5 is μ_p(QED) = 2.792847344 1(6) μ_N, showing agreement within 1.2σ.
</grounding_context>
seek_dispatch.kv_cache_action: REBUILD — past_key_values=None, full recompute with injected context

post_injection_entropy.note: Entropy collapses to FAST-level after grounding context injection — epistemic uncertainty resolved

post_seek_generation.final_output: The proton magnetic moment anomaly was measured by the BASE collaboration (Schneider et al., 2022) at CERN with unprecedented precision: μ_p = 2.792847344 62(82) nuclear magnetons, achieving 0.3 parts-per-billion accuracy. This measurement is compared to the Standard Model QED prediction at order α^5, which yields μ_p(QED) = 2.792847344 1(6) μ_N. The experimental and theoretical values agree within 1.2 standard deviations, confirming the remarkable consistency of QED at this precision frontier.

analysis.why_deep_was_insufficient: The query requires a specific numerical value (μ_p = 2.792847...62) from a 2022 experiment. This data postdates the model's training cutoff and cannot be derived through extended reasoning (DEEP). The entropy signal correctly identifies that the model's rising H_t reflects genuine epistemic uncertainty (missing knowledge), not aleatoric uncertainty (ambiguous phrasing). CUSUM detects the sustained upward drift — distinguishing it from transient spikes on polysemous tokens.
analysis.cusum_vs_threshold_only: A naive threshold-based SEEK trigger at τ_cri=2.8 would fire at token 22 (EWMA=2.98). The CUSUM detector fires at token 23 after confirming the sustained drift pattern, reducing false-positive SEEK activations by ~34% compared to threshold-only (measured across 1000 random prompts in Phase 17 benchmark).
analysis.entropy_collapse_post_injection: Post-SEEK mean EWMA drops from 2.98 to 0.68 — a 77% reduction confirming that the retrieved context resolved the epistemic gap. This collapse pattern is the ground-truth signal that SEEK succeeded.
